name: Login Help Mailer
description: Watches Mattermost audit logs for repeated login failures and emails users a Confluence password reset guide.

settings_schema.header: Repeated login failures can trigger an automatic email that points users to your Confluence password reset guide.
settings_schema.footer: Audit logging must be enabled in JSON format, and the plugin process must be able to read the configured audit log file.

settings.help_text: Turn the watcher on or off without uninstalling the plugin.
Absolute path to the Mattermost JSON audit log file, for example /var/log/mattermost_audit.log.
Send the help email after this many failed login attempts inside the configured window.
Only failed login attempts inside this rolling window are counted.
Prevents repeated help emails from being sent too often to the same user.
Internal Confluence page that explains how users should reset or recover their password.
Subject line for the help email sent to users.
How often the plugin checks the audit log for new events.
When enabled, the first startup begins at the end of the audit log to avoid replaying historical failures.
Clear stored failed-attempt counters when the user later logs in successfully.
Ignore LDAP, SAML, OIDC, and other non-local auth services. Recommended when the guide only applies to Mattermost-managed passwords.
